_meta.description: Locale-aware numeric and date parsing/formatting rules for PT-BR and EN spreadsheet environments, covering separators, currency, percentage, date order, and coercion safety.

description.en: Attempt to coerce text-formatted date cells to proper date type using locale-aware parsing cascade.
description.pt: Tenta converter celulas de data formatadas como texto para tipo data usando cascata de parsing por locale.

rules.collisionNotes: May conflict with numeric coercion if the text looks like a number (e.g., '20240315'). Date coercion should run after numeric coercion fails.

description.en: Attempt to coerce text-formatted number cells to numeric type, stripping whitespace and applying locale-aware parsing.
description.pt: Tenta converter celulas numericas formatadas como texto para tipo numerico, removendo espacos e aplicando parsing por locale.

rules.collisionNotes: Must run locale detection first to correctly interpret separator convention. Ambiguous single-separator values (e.g., '1,234') require column-level context.

description.en: Format numeric value as Brazilian Real: R$ 1.234,56 with PT-BR separators.
description.pt: Formata valor numerico como Real brasileiro: R$ 1.234,56 com separadores PT-BR.

rules.collisionNotes: Ensure currency formatting runs after numeric calculation, not before. Outputting R$ prefix prevents re-parsing unless stripped.

description.en: Parse R$ prefix and PT-BR number format: 'R$ 1.234,56' = 1234.56.
description.pt: Faz parsing do prefixo R$ e formato numerico PT-BR: 'R$ 1.234,56' = 1234.56.

rules.collisionNotes: R$ prefix is unambiguous. No collision with other currency symbols.

description.en: Format numeric value as Euro with locale-appropriate separators. PT-PT uses comma decimal; EN-GB uses dot decimal.
description.pt: Formata valor numerico como Euro com separadores apropriados ao locale. PT-PT usa virgula decimal; EN-GB usa ponto decimal.

rules.collisionNotes: Euro symbol position varies by locale. Always check locale before formatting to avoid misplacement.

description.en: Parse Euro symbol (€) prefix or suffix with locale detection for separator convention.
description.pt: Faz parsing do simbolo Euro (€) como prefixo ou sufixo com deteccao de locale para convencao de separadores.

rules.collisionNotes: Euro can appear as prefix (EN) or suffix (PT). Symbol position can hint at locale but is not reliable alone; combine with separator analysis.

description.en: Format numeric value as British Pounds: £1,234.56 with EN separators.
description.pt: Formata valor numerico como Libra esterlina: £1,234.56 com separadores EN.

rules.collisionNotes: No collision with other currency symbols. GBP always uses EN separator convention.

description.en: Parse British Pound symbol (£) prefix and EN number format.
description.pt: Faz parsing do simbolo Libra (£) como prefixo e formato numerico EN.

rules.collisionNotes: Pound symbol is unambiguous. Always assumes EN separator convention.

description.en: Format numeric value as US Dollar: $1,234.56 with EN separators.
description.pt: Formata valor numerico como Dolar americano: $1,234.56 com separadores EN.

rules.collisionNotes: Dollar sign ($) is also used by other currencies (CAD, AUD). Context or explicit currency code needed for disambiguation.

description.en: Parse $ prefix and EN number format: '$1,234.56' = 1234.56. Also handles accounting parentheses.
description.pt: Faz parsing do prefixo $ e formato numerico EN: '$1,234.56' = 1234.56. Tambem trata parenteses contabeis.

rules.collisionNotes: Dollar sign ambiguity with other dollar-denominated currencies. Accounting parens format must be detected before generic negative sign parsing.

description.en: Format date as DD/MM/YYYY for PT-BR, PT-PT, and EN-GB locales.
description.pt: Formata data como DD/MM/AAAA para locales PT-BR, PT-PT e EN-GB.

rules.collisionNotes: Output is ambiguous with MM/DD/YYYY when day <= 12. Always pair with locale metadata.

description.en: Format date as YYYY-MM-DD (ISO 8601). Universal unambiguous format for data interchange.
description.pt: Formata data como AAAA-MM-DD (ISO 8601). Formato universal nao-ambiguo para intercambio de dados.

rules.collisionNotes: No ambiguity. Preferred format for internal storage and cross-locale data transfer.

description.en: Format date in EN long form: 'January 15, 2024'.
description.pt: Formata data na forma longa EN: 'January 15, 2024'.

rules.collisionNotes: Unambiguous due to full month name. No collision risk.

description.en: Format date in PT long form: '15 de janeiro de 2024'.
description.pt: Formata data na forma longa PT: '15 de janeiro de 2024'.

rules.collisionNotes: Unambiguous due to full month name and 'de' preposition. No collision risk.

description.en: Format date as MM/DD/YYYY for EN-US locale.
description.pt: Formata data como MM/DD/AAAA para locale EN-US.

rules.collisionNotes: Ambiguous with DD/MM/YYYY when day <= 12. Only safe when locale is known to be EN-US.

description.en: Format date as month-year only: 'Jan 2024' (EN) or 'jan/2024' (PT).
description.pt: Formata data como mes-ano apenas: 'Jan 2024' (EN) ou 'jan/2024' (PT).

rules.collisionNotes: Day information is lost. Not invertible; should only be used for display, not for round-trip data.

description.en: Format date as quarter: 'Q1 2024' (EN) or '1T 2024' (PT).
description.pt: Formata data como trimestre: 'Q1 2024' (EN) ou '1T 2024' (PT).

rules.collisionNotes: Day and month precision are lost. Quarter boundaries: Q1=Jan-Mar, Q2=Apr-Jun, Q3=Jul-Sep, Q4=Oct-Dec.

description.en: Format date as relative time: '3 days ago' (EN) or 'ha 3 dias' (PT).
description.pt: Formata data como tempo relativo: '3 days ago' (EN) ou 'ha 3 dias' (PT).

rules.collisionNotes: Output depends on current date. Not stable for caching or comparison. Use only for UI display.

description.en: Format date in EN short form: 'Jan 15, 2024'.
description.pt: Formata data na forma curta EN: 'Jan 15, 2024'.

rules.collisionNotes: Unambiguous due to abbreviated month name. Safe for display.

description.en: Format date in PT short form: '15/jan/2024'.
description.pt: Formata data na forma curta PT: '15/jan/2024'.

rules.collisionNotes: Unambiguous due to abbreviated month name. Safe for display and re-parsing.

description.en: Parse DD/MM/YYYY format (PT-BR and EN-GB default). Separators: / - .
description.pt: Faz parsing do formato DD/MM/AAAA (padrao PT-BR e EN-GB). Separadores: / - .

rules.collisionNotes: Ambiguous with MM/DD/YYYY when day <= 12. Locale context required for correct parsing.

description.en: Parse '15 Jan 2024' or '15 jan 2024' with abbreviated month name in any supported language.
description.pt: Faz parsing de '15 Jan 2024' ou '15 jan 2024' com nome abreviado do mes em qualquer idioma suportado.

rules.collisionNotes: Month abbreviation 'mai' (PT May) overlaps with no EN abbreviation. 'mar' is shared between EN and PT.

description.en: Parse EN long date format: 'January 15, 2024' or 'March 1, 2024'.
description.pt: Faz parsing do formato de data longa EN: 'January 15, 2024' ou 'March 1, 2024'.

rules.collisionNotes: No collision. Full EN month names are unambiguous.

description.en: Parse PT long date format: '15 de janeiro de 2024'.
description.pt: Faz parsing do formato de data longa PT: '15 de janeiro de 2024'.

rules.collisionNotes: No collision. 'de' preposition and PT month names are unambiguous.

description.en: Parse Excel serial date number to ISO date. Excel epoch: 1900-01-01 = 1 (with Lotus 1-2-3 leap year bug for day 60).
description.pt: Converte numero serial de data do Excel para data ISO. Epoca Excel: 1900-01-01 = 1 (com bug de ano bissexto do Lotus 1-2-3 para dia 60).

rules.collisionNotes: Small integers (1-5) may be actual numeric values, not dates. Use column context or header hints to distinguish.

description.en: Parse ISO 8601 date format: YYYY-MM-DD. Highest priority as it is universally unambiguous.
description.pt: Faz parsing do formato de data ISO 8601: AAAA-MM-DD. Maior prioridade por ser universalmente nao-ambiguo.

rules.collisionNotes: No ambiguity. Year-first format is universally unambiguous. Always try this parser first.

description.en: Parse MM/DD/YYYY format (EN-US default). Separators: / - .
description.pt: Faz parsing do formato MM/DD/AAAA (padrao EN-US). Separadores: / - .

rules.collisionNotes: Ambiguous with DD/MM/YYYY when day <= 12. Must rely on locale context. If locale unknown, flag as ambiguous.

description.en: Map EN month abbreviations to month numbers: Jan=1, Feb=2, ..., Dec=12.
description.pt: Mapeia abreviacoes de meses EN para numeros: Jan=1, Feb=2, ..., Dec=12.

rules.collisionNotes: 'Mar' is the same abbreviation in EN and PT. Context is needed to determine language.

description.en: Map PT month abbreviations to month numbers: jan=1, fev=2, ..., dez=12.
description.pt: Mapeia abreviacoes de meses PT para numeros: jan=1, fev=2, ..., dez=12.

rules.collisionNotes: 'mar' overlaps with EN 'Mar'. PT abbreviations are typically lowercase which helps disambiguation.

description.en: Parse month-year without day: 'Jan 2024', 'janeiro/2024', '03/2024'. Day defaults to 1.
description.pt: Faz parsing de mes-ano sem dia: 'Jan 2024', 'janeiro/2024', '03/2024'. Dia assume valor 1.

rules.collisionNotes: Numeric format '03/2024' is ambiguous outside context; could be confused with incomplete date. Requires column-level heuristics.

description.en: Parse quarter format: 'Q1 2024' (EN) or '1T 2024' (PT). Maps to first day of quarter.
description.pt: Faz parsing do formato trimestral: 'Q1 2024' (EN) ou '1T 2024' (PT). Mapeia para primeiro dia do trimestre.

rules.collisionNotes: '1T' could theoretically match other patterns. The T suffix is specific to PT quarter notation.

description.en: Parse relative date keywords: 'today', 'yesterday', 'tomorrow' (EN) and 'hoje', 'ontem', 'amanha' (PT).
description.pt: Faz parsing de palavras-chave de data relativa: 'today', 'yesterday', 'tomorrow' (EN) e 'hoje', 'ontem', 'amanha' (PT).

rules.collisionNotes: Output is dynamic and depends on evaluation date. Not suitable for cached or static contexts.

description.en: Parse ISO week number format: 'W05' or 'Sem 05'. Maps to Monday of that ISO week.
description.pt: Faz parsing do formato de numero de semana ISO: 'W05' ou 'Sem 05'. Mapeia para segunda-feira daquela semana ISO.

rules.collisionNotes: Week number without year is ambiguous. 'Sem' may collide with Portuguese word 'sem' (without) in text cells.

description.en: Parse standalone 4-digit year (e.g., '2024'). Maps to January 1 of that year.
description.pt: Faz parsing de ano isolado com 4 digitos (ex: '2024'). Mapeia para 1 de janeiro daquele ano.

rules.collisionNotes: 4-digit numbers in range 1900-2099 could be actual numeric values. Use column header context to determine if year or number.

description.en: EN accounting format: currency-aligned with parentheses for negatives, e.g., '$ 1,234.56' and '$(1,234.56)'.
description.pt: Formato contabil EN: alinhado por moeda com parenteses para negativos, ex: '$ 1,234.56' e '$(1,234.56)'.

rules.collisionNotes: Accounting format is distinct from plain currency format by alignment and spacing. Parentheses negatives must be parsed before generic number parsing.

description.en: PT-BR accounting format: R$ with dot thousands and comma decimal, e.g., 'R$ 1.234,56'.
description.pt: Formato contabil PT-BR: R$ com ponto para milhares e virgula decimal, ex: 'R$ 1.234,56'.

rules.collisionNotes: PT-BR does not typically use parentheses for negatives in accounting. Uses minus sign prefix instead.

description.en: Format number with comma as decimal separator and dot as thousands for PT-BR/PT-PT output.
description.pt: Formata numero com virgula como separador decimal e ponto como separador de milhares para saida PT-BR/PT-PT.

rules.collisionNotes: Ensure this formatter is only applied to PT locale output contexts. Applying to EN data will produce incorrect results.

description.en: Format large numbers in compact notation: K (thousands), M (millions), B (billions). E.g., 1200000 = '1.2M'.
description.pt: Formata numeros grandes em notacao compacta: K (milhares), M (milhoes), B (bilhoes). Ex: 1200000 = '1.2M'.

rules.collisionNotes: Compact notation loses precision. Not suitable for financial or exact-value contexts. Suffix letters may conflict with cell content.

description.en: Enforce a specific number of decimal places via rounding. Configurable from 0 to 10.
description.pt: Aplica numero especifico de casas decimais via arredondamento. Configuravel de 0 a 10.

rules.collisionNotes: Rounding mode affects values at exact midpoints (e.g., 2.5). 'halfUp' rounds 2.5 to 3; 'halfEven' (banker's rounding) rounds to 2.

description.en: Format number with dot as decimal separator and comma as thousands for EN output.
description.pt: Formata numero com ponto como separador decimal e virgula como separador de milhares para saida EN.

rules.collisionNotes: Ensure this formatter is only applied to EN locale output contexts. Applying to PT data will produce incorrect results.

description.en: Format negative numbers using parentheses instead of minus sign: (1,234.56).
description.pt: Formata numeros negativos usando parenteses em vez de sinal de menos: (1,234.56).

rules.collisionNotes: Parentheses notation is primarily an accounting convention in EN locales. Not standard in PT-BR formatting.

description.en: Format negative numbers in red color. Applied as a cell style, not text transformation.
description.pt: Formata numeros negativos em cor vermelha. Aplicado como estilo de celula, nao como transformacao de texto.

rules.collisionNotes: Red formatting is a visual style only. It does not affect the numeric value. May conflict with conditional formatting rules.

description.en: Format number as percentage with specified decimal places. Input is expected as decimal (0.45 = 45%).
description.pt: Formata numero como porcentagem com casas decimais especificadas. Entrada esperada como decimal (0.45 = 45%).

rules.collisionNotes: Input scale matters: 0.45 vs 45 both could represent 45%. Use pct_normalize rules to determine input scale.

description.en: Format number in scientific notation: 1.23E+04. Used for very large or very small values.
description.pt: Formata numero em notacao cientifica: 1.23E+04. Usado para valores muito grandes ou muito pequenos.

rules.collisionNotes: Scientific notation may be misinterpreted as text by downstream consumers. Mark cells formatted this way with appropriate metadata.

description.en: Parse accounting-style parentheses as negative numbers: '(1,234.56)' = -1234.56.
description.pt: Faz parsing de parenteses no estilo contabil como numeros negativos: '(1,234.56)' = -1234.56.

rules.collisionNotes: Parentheses wrapping is a convention in EN accounting. Must be checked before generic number parsing to avoid treating parens as text.

description.en: In PT-BR, comma is decimal separator: '1.234,56' = 1234.56.
description.pt: No PT-BR, virgula e separador decimal: '1.234,56' = 1234.56.

rules.collisionNotes: Ambiguous with EN thousands format when only one separator present (e.g., '1,234' could be PT decimal or EN thousands).

description.en: In EN, comma is thousands separator: '1,234,567' = 1234567.
description.pt: No EN, virgula e separador de milhares: '1,234,567' = 1234567.

rules.collisionNotes: Ambiguous with PT-BR decimal when only one comma present (e.g., '1,234' could be EN thousands or PT value 1.234).

description.en: Strip currency prefix symbols ($, R$, EUR, GBP) before numeric parsing.
description.pt: Remove simbolos de moeda como prefixo ($, R$, EUR, GBP) antes do parsing numerico.

rules.collisionNotes: After stripping, the remaining string still needs locale-aware numeric parsing. Currency symbol provides a strong locale hint.

description.en: Strip currency code suffix (BRL, USD, EUR, GBP) before numeric parsing.
description.pt: Remove codigo de moeda como sufixo (BRL, USD, EUR, GBP) antes do parsing numerico.

rules.collisionNotes: Currency code suffixes are unambiguous (ISO 4217). They provide a reliable locale hint for subsequent numeric parsing.

description.en: Treat a dash or hyphen cell ('-' or '--') as numeric zero. Common in financial spreadsheets for empty/zero values.
description.pt: Trata celula com traco ou hifen ('-' ou '--') como zero numerico. Comum em planilhas financeiras para valores vazios/zero.

rules.collisionNotes: A single dash could be a text placeholder, not necessarily zero. Use column context (numeric column with other numbers) to validate.

description.en: In EN, dot is decimal separator: '1234.56' = 1234.56.
description.pt: No EN, ponto e separador decimal: '1234.56' = 1234.56.

rules.collisionNotes: No thousands separator in this pattern. A value like '1.234' could be EN decimal 1.234 or start of PT thousands notation.

description.en: In PT-BR, dot is thousands separator: '1.234.567' = 1234567.
description.pt: No PT-BR, ponto e separador de milhares: '1.234.567' = 1234567.

rules.collisionNotes: A value like '1.234' is ambiguous: PT thousands (1234) vs EN decimal (1.234). Multiple dot groups (1.234.567) are unambiguous PT thousands.

description.en: Auto-detect separator convention by analyzing column data. If a column has both '1,234.56' and '1.234,56', flag as mixed and require locale resolution.
description.pt: Detecta automaticamente convencao de separadores analisando dados da coluna. Se coluna tem '1,234.56' e '1.234,56', marca como misto e requer resolucao de locale.

rules.collisionNotes: Mixed-locale columns are a data quality issue. Always flag to the user rather than silently choosing one convention.

description.en: Parse plain integer or decimal without any thousands separator: '12345' or '123.45' / '123,45'.
description.pt: Faz parsing de numero inteiro ou decimal simples sem separador de milhares: '12345' ou '123.45' / '123,45'.

rules.collisionNotes: Single separator is ambiguous without locale context. '123,45' is PT decimal; '123.45' is EN decimal. Both produce 123.45 but for different locale reasons.

description.en: Strip '%' symbol and divide by 100 to get decimal representation: '45.6%' = 0.456.
description.pt: Remove simbolo '%' e divide por 100 para obter representacao decimal: '45.6%' = 0.456.

rules.collisionNotes: The numeric part before % still requires locale-aware parsing (e.g., '12,5%' is PT for 12.5%). Always parse the number after stripping %.

description.en: Parse space as thousands separator: '1 234 567' = 1234567. Used in some European and ISO formats.
description.pt: Faz parsing de espaco como separador de milhares: '1 234 567' = 1234567. Usado em alguns formatos europeus e ISO.

rules.collisionNotes: Space-separated numbers are less common in Excel but appear in imported data. Regular spaces and non-breaking spaces should both be handled.

description.en: Detect percentage column from header keywords (e.g., 'rate', 'margin', 'growth', '%') or presence of '%' symbol in data values.
description.pt: Detecta coluna de porcentagem por palavras-chave no cabecalho (ex: 'taxa', 'margem', 'crescimento', '%') ou presenca do simbolo '%' nos dados.

rules.collisionNotes: Header keywords like 'rate' could refer to interest rates (which may already be in decimal form). Combine header and data analysis.

description.en: Normalize percentage from 0-100 display scale to 0-1 calculation scale: 45.6 -> 0.456.
description.pt: Normaliza porcentagem da escala de exibicao 0-100 para escala de calculo 0-1: 45.6 -> 0.456.

rules.collisionNotes: Must determine input scale before normalizing. A value of 0.45 could already be in 0-1 scale; dividing again would produce 0.0045.

description.en: Normalize percentage from 0-1 calculation scale to 0-100 display scale: 0.456 -> 45.6.
description.pt: Normaliza porcentagem da escala de calculo 0-1 para escala de exibicao 0-100: 0.456 -> 45.6.

rules.collisionNotes: Must determine input scale before normalizing. A value of 45 could already be in 0-100 scale; multiplying again would produce 4500.

description.en: Parse percentage value with % symbol, respecting locale decimal separator: '45.6%' (EN) or '45,6%' (PT).
description.pt: Faz parsing de valor percentual com simbolo %, respeitando separador decimal do locale: '45.6%' (EN) ou '45,6%' (PT).

rules.collisionNotes: Decimal separator ambiguity applies: '45,6%' is 45.6% in PT but would be invalid in EN. Use locale context.

description.en: Analyze full column values to determine separator convention. Majority vote across all numeric cells in the column.
description.pt: Analisa todos os valores da coluna para determinar convencao de separadores. Voto majoritario entre todas as celulas numericas da coluna.

rules.collisionNotes: Columns with few values or only ambiguous values may produce low confidence. Combine with other detection methods.

description.en: Use frequency analysis of '.' and ',' in numeric cells to detect locale. EN: more commas as thousands; PT: more dots as thousands.
description.pt: Usa analise de frequencia de '.' e ',' em celulas numericas para detectar locale. EN: mais virgulas como milhares; PT: mais pontos como milhares.

action.heuristic: If comma count > dot count in non-decimal positions, likely EN. If dot count > comma count, likely PT.

rules.collisionNotes: Frequency analysis is a heuristic and can fail with small samples. Best used as a tiebreaker alongside other methods.

description.en: Use the sheet's locale setting, OS locale, or explicit user preference as the primary separator convention hint.
description.pt: Usa a configuracao de locale da planilha, locale do SO ou preferencia explicita do usuario como dica principal de convencao de separadores.

rules.collisionNotes: Locale hints can be wrong if the data was created in a different locale than the current environment. Always validate against actual data.

description.en: Use digit grouping position to disambiguate: groups of exactly 3 digits after separator indicate thousands. E.g., '1,234' has 3-digit group = thousands.
description.pt: Usa posicao de agrupamento de digitos para desambiguar: grupos de exatamente 3 digitos apos separador indicam milhares. Ex: '1,234' tem grupo de 3 digitos = milhares.

rules.collisionNotes: The 3-digit rule fails for exactly 3 decimal places (e.g., '1.234' could be EN decimal 1.234 or PT thousands 1234). Combine with other methods.

description.en: Reject dates outside reasonable range 1900-2100. Prevents nonsensical dates from serial number miscalculation.
description.pt: Rejeita datas fora do intervalo razoavel 1900-2100. Previne datas sem sentido de erros de calculo de numeros seriais.

rules.collisionNotes: The 1900 lower bound matches Excel's epoch. Dates before 1900 cannot be represented as Excel serial numbers.

description.en: Warn when negative values appear in columns that typically hold non-negative data (e.g., quantities, counts, ages).
description.pt: Alerta quando valores negativos aparecem em colunas que tipicamente contem dados nao-negativos (ex: quantidades, contagens, idades).

rules.collisionNotes: Some domains allow negative quantities (e.g., inventory adjustments, returns). This is a soft warning, not a hard rejection.

description.en: Detect values exceeding Excel's maximum precision of 15 significant digits. Values beyond this are silently rounded by Excel.
description.pt: Detecta valores que excedem a precisao maxima do Excel de 15 digitos significativos. Valores alem disso sao arredondados silenciosamente pelo Excel.

rules.collisionNotes: This is especially critical for ID columns (e.g., credit card numbers, CPF/CNPJ) which may lose trailing digits when treated as numbers.

description.en: Warn when a column contains mixed numeric and text values, indicating potential data quality issues.
description.pt: Alerta quando uma coluna contem valores mistos numerico e texto, indicando potenciais problemas de qualidade de dados.

rules.collisionNotes: Some mixed columns are intentional (e.g., 'N/A', '-', 'TBD' as placeholders). Consider coercion rules before flagging.
